items.note: Cone template tuned for high clarity and even drawdown.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.note: Saturate every dry pocket and let the bed dome slightly.

steps.label: Main Pour
steps.note: Pour in a steady spiral to maintain a centered bed.

steps.label: Finish Pour
steps.note: Finish cleanly, then let the brew settle into drawdown.

items.note: Japanese iced variant using concentrated hot water over measured ice; service/stir happens after drawdown, not as a brew pour.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.note: Use a compact bloom to wet every dry pocket and stabilize extraction.

steps.label: Center Pour
steps.note: Build to roughly half of the hot-water target with a centered stream.

steps.label: Second Pulse
steps.note: Keep slurry depth modest and avoid washing the paper wall.

steps.label: Final Pour
steps.note: Finish to the hot-water target, then let drawdown finish over the ice before stirring.

items.note: Flat-bottom template focused on broad sweetness and stable bed depth.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.note: Widen the bloom enough to wet the full bed.

steps.label: Pulse One
steps.note: Pulse evenly without chasing the filter walls.

steps.label: Pulse Two
steps.note: Let the bed flatten before the last pour.

steps.label: Finish Pour
steps.note: Finish centrally so the bed stays level into drawdown.

items.note: Iced flat-bottom template to keep sweetness while preserving sparkle.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.note: Wet evenly and keep the filter walls clean.

steps.label: Pulse One
steps.note: Pulse with a controlled spiral, avoiding aggressive turbulence.

steps.label: Finish Pour
steps.note: Finish to the hot-water target and chill immediately over ice.

items.note: Chemex template accounting for the higher resistance paper and slower drain.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.note: Use enough water to fully swell the thicker paper-filter bed.

steps.label: First Main Pour
steps.note: Build the first body of water without flooding the cone.

steps.label: Second Main Pour
steps.note: Keep the kettle close and maintain a calm spiral.

steps.label: Finish Pour
steps.note: Finish cleanly and let the paper drain without stirring.

items.note: Chemex iced template emphasizing a controlled, lower-agitation brew bed.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.note: Give the thicker paper enough time to preheat and stabilize.

steps.label: First Main Pour
steps.note: Pour steadily to avoid stalling the thick paper.

steps.label: Finish Pour
steps.note: Finish to target and chill immediately over ice.

items.note: Origami template that keeps agility but stays close to cone-dripper clarity.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.note: Open the bed evenly before the first main pour.

steps.label: Main Pour
steps.note: Pour with moderate height to keep the bed active but controlled.

steps.label: Third Pour
steps.note: Reset the bed level before finishing.

steps.label: Finish Pour
steps.note: Finish centrally and let the filter do the cleanup.

items.note: Origami iced template with crisp aromatics and quick dilution control.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.note: Let the bed release gas before the larger pulses.

steps.label: Main Pour
steps.note: Use a smooth, narrow spiral to preserve brightness.

steps.label: Finish Pour
steps.note: Finish and chill immediately over the measured ice dose.

items.note: April-style lower agitation flat-bottom template with calm extraction pacing.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.note: Wet the bed quickly without stirring the slurry.

steps.label: Second Pour
steps.note: Pour centrally to keep the drawdown calm and even.

steps.label: Third Pour
steps.note: Keep the flow steady and the kettle close to the bed.

steps.label: Finish Pour
steps.note: Finish with minimal agitation and allow the flat bed to settle.

items.note: April-style iced template tuned for sweetness and clean dilution.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.note: Fully wet the bed, then let the gas release naturally.

steps.label: Second Pour
steps.note: Add the second pulse with the kettle kept low and centered.

steps.label: Finish Pour
steps.note: Finish to target and pour onto the waiting ice immediately.

items.note: Immersion template using one full charge and a controlled release.

steps.label: Full Fill
steps.note: Add the full brew water, stir lightly once, then steep.

steps.label: Steep
steps.note: Let the slurry settle with the valve closed.

steps.label: Release Drawdown
steps.note: Set the brewer on the server and let it draw down cleanly.

items.note: Immersion iced template using a shorter steep and a direct serve over ice.

steps.label: Full Fill
steps.note: Add the full hot-water charge and stir once to break clumps.

steps.label: Steep
steps.note: Hold the slurry quiet so the cup stays clean when chilled.

steps.label: Release Drawdown
steps.note: Draw directly onto the measured ice for immediate chilling.
